long_stay: Official language courses (minimum 20 hours/week, certified by Instituto Cervantes)
Primary or secondary school in Spain
Volunteering programmes organised by recognised organisations
Higher Vocational Training (Formación Profesional or FP)
Approved student exchange programs lasting more than 90 days
qualifying_courses.notes: The student visa is granted for programmes officially recognised by the Spanish Ministry of Education. You can search your institution in the RUCT (Registry of Universities, Centres and Degrees) to confirm recognition. Language schools must be recognized by Instituto Cervantes.

types.name: Long-stay Study Visa (Visado de Estudios)
types.notes: The visa sticker in your passport is valid for 90 days for entry. Any other type of visa (e.g., tourist/Schengen) does not entitle you to the subsequent processing of your Student Residence Card (TIE), and you would have to leave Spain after three months.

booking_methods.description: Schedule an in-person appointment directly through the online system of the specific Spanish Embassy or Consulate holding jurisdiction over your region.

In several regions (e.g., parts of the US, UK, and India), Spain outsources its visa intake processing to external networks such as BLS International. You will need to book your biometric appointment through their portal.

steps.title: Get accepted and obtain admission letter
steps.description: Get accepted at a recognised Spanish educational institution and obtain your official admission letter.

steps.title: Book consulate appointment
steps.description: Book your consulate appointment as early as possible. Applications must be submitted at least two months before the start of the programme.

steps.title: Prepare documents
steps.description: Prepare and gather all required documents.

steps.title: Attend appointment and pay fee
steps.description: Attend your appointment in person, submit documents, and pay the visa fee.

steps.title: Wait for decision
steps.description: Wait for a decision from the consulate.

steps.title: Receive visa sticker
steps.description: Once approved, you receive a visa sticker in your passport (valid 90 days for entry).

steps.title: Travel to Spain
steps.description: Travel to Spain before the visa expires.

steps.title: Apply for TIE
steps.description: Within 30 days of arrival, apply for your TIE (residence card) at the local police station.

required_documents.name: Visa Application Form
required_documents.details: National visa form, completed and signed, available from the Consulate's website.

required_documents.name: Passport-sized Photographs
required_documents.details: Two recent, passport-size, colour photographs with a white background.

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: Original and copy of photo page and visa page. Must be valid for at least 1 year, issued within the last 10 years, and have 2 blank pages.

required_documents.name: Academic Transcripts
required_documents.details: Previously completed academic transcripts. The Apostille of the Hague is required for some countries.

required_documents.name: Official Admission Letter
required_documents.details: In Spanish, from an authorized Spanish educational institution, stating program details, start/end dates. Must show at least 20 hours/week for language courses.

required_documents.name: Medical Insurance
required_documents.details: Comprehensive private health insurance with a Spanish provider. Must feature €0 deductibles/co-payments and full hospitalization coverage. Must be valid from 1 month before until 15 days after program end.

required_documents.name: Criminal Record Certificate
required_documents.details: A clean background check from your home country, covering the last 5 years. Must be Apostilled/Legalized and translated into Spanish by an official sworn translator (traductor jurado).

required_documents.name: Proof of Accommodation
required_documents.details: Letter of invitation, rental agreement, student dorm certificate, or hotel booking for your first few weeks combined with a letter of intent.

required_documents.name: Medical Certificate
required_documents.details: A signed statement from a doctor confirming you do not suffer from any illnesses that pose a public health risk under International Health Regulations.

required_documents.name: Tuition Payment Proof
required_documents.details: Proof of having paid registration/enrollment fees. This is a new requirement under Royal Decree 1155/2024.

financial_requirements.reference_basis: IPREM (Indicador Público de Renta de Efectos Múltiples)
acceptable_proof: Bank statements showing regular transactions and a stable balance over the last 3–6 months
An official document proving a fully funded scholarship
Notarized letter of sponsorship from a parent or guardian, alongside their personal income tax filings and stamped bank certificates
exemptions: The requirement can be reduced if accommodation is fully paid in advance for the entire stay

residence_permit.booking_method: Online at the police station's website (select province, then 'Expedición de Tarjeta de Identidad de Extranjeros (huella)')
required_documents: Passport (original and photocopy of photo page and visa page)
Tax receipt for payment of TASA 790-012
residence_permit.notes: The NIE (Número de Identidad de Extranjero) is your foreigner identification number, usually assigned during your visa process and appears on your visa sticker. The TIE (Tarjeta de Identidad de Extranjero) is your physical residence card. The physical card may take 2–3 months to receive. Until issued, the Resguardo (stamped temporary document) serves as your legal residence permit. Under 2025 reforms, the student residence permit is now valid for the entire duration of the academic programme for university students. If your course extends or you change institution, a modification or renewal may be needed.

restrictions: The 30-hour work right applies to higher education students only (enrolled in university degree programmes, master's, or vocational training (FP))
Language school students do not have this automatic right; their employer may need to register a separate part-time work authorization
Work hours must not conflict with your class timetable
Income cannot be declared as your main source of survival when applying for or renewing your visa
Working more than 30 hours per week or allowing employment to interfere with academic obligations constitutes a breach of study visa conditions
work_while_studying.notes: The right to work is built into the student residence permit and does not require a separate work permit. Students can work full-time (40 hours/week) during official holidays (summer, Christmas).

permit_renewal.online_portal: Sede Electrónica of the Ministry of the Interior or government's MERCURIO portal
required_documents: Proof of passing previous courses (certificate of aprovechamiento or new enrollment)
Fresh bank statement meeting the €600/month IPREM rule
permit_renewal.notes: Under the 2025 reforms, the student residence permit is now valid for the entire duration of the academic programme for university students, eliminating the need for annual renewals. If your course extends or you change institution, you will need to apply for a modification or renewal from within Spain, either online or in person at the Oficina de Extranjería, well in advance of your current permit's expiry date.

post_study_work.legal_basis: Job Search Authorisation (Autorización de Búsqueda de Empleo)
requirements: Apply before your student residence expires, either during the 60 days before expiry or within 90 days after expiry

Upon securing an official job contract related to your studies, you can modify your student status directly to an Employee Residence and Work Permit (Autorización de Residencia y Trabajo por Cuenta Ajena) or an EU Blue Card / Highly Skilled Professional Permit
post_study_work.notes: The Job Search Authorisation is valid for a minimum of 12 months and a maximum of 24 months, with no possibility of extension, giving you up to two years to find a job or start a company.

official_sources.name: RUCT (Registry of Universities, Centres and Degrees)
Spanish Ministry of Education, Vocational Training and Sports
official_sources.description: General student portal and educational directory

application_forms.name: Form EX-00
application_forms.authority: Ministry of Inclusion, Social Security and Migration or your local consulate’s web pages

tips.title: Plan your appointment early
tips.body: Plan your embassy appointment at least 4 weeks before your planned departure date to Spain. To allow for unforeseen formalities or requests, 6 to 8 weeks is ideal.

tips.title: Do not purchase travel tickets prematurely
tips.body: Consulates strongly recommend not purchasing travel tickets until the visa is granted.

tips.title: Verify latest requirements
tips.body: Always verify the latest requirements with your nearest Spanish consulate or at https://www.exteriores.gob.es before applying, especially due to significant updates by Royal Decree 1155/2024, effective May 20, 2025.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying for a tourist/Schengen visa instead of a student visa
common_mistakes.consequence: Any other type of visa does not entitle you to the TIE, and you would have to leave Spain after three months.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure you apply for a student visa (Visado de Estudios).

common_mistakes.mistake: Trying to apply for a study visa from within Spain
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Since May 20, 2025, all study visa applications must be made through the Spanish consulate in your home country.

common_mistakes.consequence: All documents must be in Spanish or they will not be accepted.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Have all foreign documents officially translated by a sworn (jurado) translator.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying too late for the visa
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Applications must be submitted at least two months before the start of the programme; starting 3–6 months early is recommended.
Apply for the TIE within 30 days of arriving in Spain at the Oficina de Extranjería.

common_mistakes.consequence: It is required for your TIE and for accessing local services.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Register at your local Ayuntamiento immediately after arrival.

common_mistakes.mistake: Assuming all students can work 30 hours/week
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: The 30-hour rule applies to higher education students only; language school students do not have this automatic right.
Your NIE is a number; your TIE is the physical card you must actively apply for after arrival.

common_mistakes.mistake: Delaying the job search permit application
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply during the 60 days before your student residence permit expires, or within 90 days after expiry; do not wait until the last moment.

common_mistakes.consequence: If your private policy contains a co-payment (copago) clause or a deductible of even €5, your visa will be rejected.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Always ask your broker for a dedicated policy designed specifically for the 'Spanish Student Visa' with zero co-pays.

common_mistakes.consequence: Standard translations from normal agencies or apps are rejected.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Any document not written in Spanish (criminal background records, bank letters, diplomas) must be translated by an officially recognized Spanish Sworn Translator (Traductor Jurado).

common_mistakes.consequence: Finding an open slot (cita previa) for your TIE card in major cities like Madrid or Barcelona can be incredibly difficult due to system backlogs.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Start checking the government booking site the week you arrive in the country.
